english: lág-pa-nas (lag pa nas) to grasp a person's hand Mil.; mgó-nas (mgo nas) taking hold of a skull Dzl. 22, 6; gós-kyi mťá-ma (gos kyi mtha' ma) to seize the coat-tail Dzl.; mí (mi) a man, = to catch, frq.; čúṅ-mar (chung mar) _dzin-pa ('dzin pa) to take wives Glr.; to hold, lág-na (lag na) rál-gri (ral gri) to hold a sword in one's hand Glr.; *Kyi zum toṅ* W., *Kyi dzin (or zin) rog jhe* C., hold the dog fast! to catch, a ball, rain-water etc.; bzuṅ-bas (bzung bas) mi (mi) zin (zin) capiendo non capitur, it (the soul) cannot be taken hold of Mil.; bdág-gi (bdag gi) gduṅ-brgyúd (gdung brgyud) _dzin-pai ('dzin pa'i) rgyál-bu (rgyal bu) a prince upholding my race Glr.; to hold, support, a certain doctrine; to embrace, another religion Glr., v. below; to take upon one's self, some religious duty. — 2. to get, receive, obtain. — 3. to occupy, to take possession of, hold in possession, a country Ma., rgyál-sa (rgyal sa) the throne; to be seized, nád-kyis (nad kyis) zin-pa (zin pa) seized with a malady Mil., — 4. intellectually: to take in, comprehend, grasp, conceive, by the faculty of perception or imagination: dbáṅ-po-rnams-kyi (dbang po rnams kyi) nús-pa (nus pa) zad-pas (zad pas) yul (yul) mi (mi) _dzin-pa-am ('dzin pa am) gźán-du (gzhan du) _dzin-pa ('dzin pa) to perceive things not as they are, or not at all, in consequence of weakened senses Thgy.; with reference to mind or memory: séms-la (sems la), yíd-la (yid la), bló-la (blo la) B. and col.; to be taken in, affected, seized, captivated, sdíg-pas (sdig pas) zin-pa (zin pa) to be affected, taken, by sin Mil.; tŭgs-rjés (thugs rjes) zin-pa (zin pa) to be kindly, graciously, affected towards a person; ťugs-ma (thugs ma) zin-pa (zin pa) to be not graciously inclined Mil. nt.; bú-mos (bu mos) zin-pa (zin pa) taken in love with a girl Pth.; _dzin-pa ('dzin pa) ťams-čád (thams cad) all that captivates me; to choose, to follow, rí-kród (ri khrod) to choose the solitude of mountains Mil., dmán-sa (dman sa) to follow humility, to choose lowliness Mil. and elsewh.; to embrace, another religion, v. above; to take for, to consider, esteem, ṅa-la (nga la) dgrar (dgrar) taking me for an enemy Dzl.; mi (mi) or mí-la (mi la) gčès-par (gces par) or sdúg-par (sdug par) to value, esteem, love, a person, v. gčès-pa (gces pa); p̌ar (phar), mar (mar) to esteem, respect one, as a father, as a mother Stg.; méd-pa-la (med pa la) yód-par (yod par) to consider the not existing as existing Thgr.; gnyís-su (gnyis su) to consider as different, to find a difference between two things, which according to Buddhist philosophy are one and the same, cf. gnyís-_dzín (gnyis 'dzin); also absolutely, without an object being mentioned: dṅós-por (dngos por) _dzín-pa ('dzin pa) to believe in the reality (of a thing) Mil. — 5. rjés-su (rjes su) _dzín-pa ('dzin pa) v. rjès (rjes). II. sbst. 1. he that seizes, holds, occupies, rígs-sṅags (rigs sngags) _dzin-pa ('dzin pa) the holder of a magic sentence; adherent, keeper etc. — 2. that which affects, captivates, in an intellectual sense, v. above _dzín-pa ('dzin pa) ťams-čád (thams cad); the being seized or affected with, or as we should say, taking an interest in, v. sub spóṅ-ba (spong ba); also cf. gzuṅ-_dzín (gzung 'dzin). — _dzín-skyóṅ ('dzin skyong), ṕo-bráṅ (pho brang) _dii ('di'i) _dzin-skyóṅ ('dzin skyong) gyis (gyis) occupy this palace and take care of it Glr. — _dzín-pa ('dzin pa) the earth, as a receptacle of beings Sch.
Lt.? acc. to one Lex. = _dzin-pa ('dzin pa).
= འཛག་པ་ dzág-pa (dzag pa) to drop, to drip Lex.

and ཟུག་པ་ _dzugs-pa ('dzugs pa) and zug-pa (zug pa), pf. btsugs (btsugs), zugs (zugs), fut. gzugs (gzugs), imp. zug(s) (zug(s)), (trs. to _tsugs-pa ('tsugs pa)) 1. to prick or stick into, to set, to prick a stick, to set a plant, into the ground, to plant, frq.; to run, thrust, pierce, to run one's self a splinter into the flesh etc. W.; to erect, a pillar, to raise, a standard. — 2. to put down, to place, a kettle Dzl.; to place before, mí-la (mi la) ṕór-pa (phor pa) to place a drinking-bowl before a person (more genteel than bźag-pa (bzhag pa)) Glr.; to put or place on, to touch with, mdzúb-mo (mdzub mo) the finger; esp. pús-mo(-i lha-ṅá) (pus mo(-i lha nga)) sá-la (sa la) to place the knee on the ground, to kneel down, v. pús-mo (pus mo); źábs-_dzugs-kyi (zhabs 'dzugs kyi) dga-ston (dga' ston) feast given, when a little child begins to walk Glr. — 3. to lay out, a garden, to found, a town, a convent; to institute, a sacrificial festival Glr.; to introduce, srol (srol) a custom Lex., hence in a general sense, to begin, commence, any business, with or without mgo (mgo); *ku-rim tsúg-sa ma tsugs* W. has the ceremony already begun? is it a going? rgól-ba (rgol ba) _dzúgs-pa ('dzugs pa) to offer resistance Pth. — 4. to prick, sting, pierce, mdas (mdas) with arrows Dzl.; fig. mí-ka (mi ka) zúg-pa (zug pa) hurting by malicious words Do. :
